name: Catalog-item macro substitution — conformance fixtures
description: Reference vectors for the NFC normalization + percent-encoding + nested-expansion rules on buyer-controlled catalog-item macros. Sales agents SHOULD reproduce each `expected` output bit-exactly. All `value` fields are as-supplied (may include NFD); the substitution pipeline MUST normalize to NFC before encoding per docs/creative/universal-macros#substitution-safety-catalog-item-macros. Vectors cover: (1) reserved-character URL breakout, (2) nested-expansion literal preservation, (3) CRLF injection, (4) non-ASCII UTF-8 encoding (NFC input), (5) NFC normalization from NFD input, (6) mixed path/query substitution, (7) bidi-override neutralization, (8) URL-scheme injection neutralization, (9-11) content-macro values (free-text title with ampersand and spaces, double-brace downstream-macro neutralization in a content value, NFD content title).

vectors.name: reserved-character-breakout
vectors.description: A catalog value containing `&` and `=` would break out of the surrounding query string if substituted raw. Percent-encoding neutralizes the breakout.

vectors.name: nested-expansion-preserved-as-literal
vectors.description: A catalog value containing AdCP macro syntax `{DEVICE_ID}` MUST NOT be re-expanded. The braces are percent-encoded; the inner token survives as a literal.

vectors.name: crlf-injection-neutralized
vectors.description: A catalog value containing CR/LF would enable request-header smuggling or log-row injection in downstream consumers. The unreserved-whitelist rule escapes both bytes, defeating the vector.

vectors.name: non-ascii-utf8-percent-encoding
vectors.description: Non-ASCII octets MUST be percent-encoded after UTF-8 encoding per RFC 3986 §2.5. A value containing `café` emits `%C3%A9` for the `é`.

vectors.name: mixed-path-and-query-contexts
vectors.description: Verifies encoding applies equally to path segments and query strings within the same URL.

vectors.name: bidi-override-neutralized
vectors.description: Unicode bidi override characters (U+202E, U+2066-U+2069) in catalog values can spoof audit-log rendering. The unreserved-whitelist rule percent-encodes them as their UTF-8 bytes.

vectors.name: nfc-normalization-before-encoding
vectors.description: Catalog value in Unicode NFD (base `e` U+0065 + combining acute U+0301) MUST be normalized to NFC (single codepoint U+00E9) BEFORE percent-encoding. Without normalization, the NFD form would encode to `e%CC%81` instead of the NFC form's `%C3%A9`. Two implementations that both satisfy the unreserved-whitelist rule diverge if they don't share a normalization form. NFC is the web-platform convention (WHATWG URL, HTML5 DOM). The `value` field below shows the NFD input (`cafe\u0301-amsterdam`); implementations that skip NFC normalization fail this vector.

vectors.name: url-scheme-injection-neutralized
vectors.description: A catalog value containing `javascript:alert(0)` substituted into an href-whole-value position (e.g., `<a href="{CLICK}">`) would otherwise execute as a javascript: scheme URL. The strict unreserved-whitelist rule percent-encodes the colon and parens so the browser parses the result as a relative URL against the base, neutralizing the injection. Parentheses `(` and `)` are NOT in RFC 3986 `unreserved` (they are sub-delims), so the strict rule encodes them — encodeURIComponent-based encoders that leave parens alone FAIL this vector, which is how the runtime observer (#2638) distinguishes strict-RFC-3986 encoders from permissive ones.

vectors.name: content-macro-freetext-title
vectors.description: A content macro carries free-text catalog field values (here {ITEM_NAME}) that are far more likely than ID values to contain spaces and reserved characters. The value `Tom & Jerry — 50% off` must percent-encode the space, ampersand, em-dash (non-ASCII, UTF-8), and percent sign under the same unreserved-whitelist rule as ID macros. Content macros are scalar text in a URL context — no new escaping context.

vectors.name: content-macro-double-brace-neutralized
vectors.description: A content value containing the downstream ad-server double-brace macro syntax `{{store.name}}` MUST be neutralized. AdCP performs a single substitution pass and does NOT adopt `{{...}}` as a content-macro syntax; percent-encoding the value escapes the braces (`{` and `}` are outside the unreserved set), so the literal survives without being interpreted by AdCP or a downstream ad server reading this AdCP-emitted URL.

vectors.name: content-macro-nfc-from-nfd-title
vectors.description: A content-macro value supplied in Unicode NFD (`Café Noir`, base `e` U+0065 + combining acute U+0301) MUST be normalized to NFC (`Café Noir`, U+00E9) before percent-encoding, identical to the ID-macro NFC rule. The NFC form emits `%C3%A9`; an implementation that skips normalization emits `e%CC%81` and fails this vector.
